title: Empty statements should be removed
description: Empty statements should be removed. Empty statements represented by a semicolon (;) are statements that do not perform any operation. They are often the result of a typo or a misunderstanding of the language syntax. It is a good practice to remove empty statements since they don't add value and lead to confusion and errors. This enhanced rule detects various forms of empty statements including standalone semicolons, empty control structure bodies, consecutive semicolons, empty case statements, and empty labeled statements. Exception: Intentionally empty statements with explanatory comments, empty macro expansions, and certain debug/assertion macros are not flagged.
recommendation: Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.

checks.message: Consecutive empty statements detected. Remove unnecessary semicolons.
Standalone empty statement detected. Remove unnecessary semicolon.
Empty control structure body detected. Add meaningful code or use block with comment.
Empty case statement detected. Add code or fall-through comment.
Empty else clause detected. Remove or add meaningful code.
Empty do-while body detected. Add meaningful code.
Empty labeled statement detected. Add meaningful code after label.
Empty statement in class/struct body detected. Remove unnecessary semicolon.
Empty statement detected. Review and remove if unnecessary.
